title: How to use Grok: a beginner’s guide
lede: Grok is xAI's assistant built into X, known for real-time information and a casual tone. Here's how to start using it, what it's best at, and how to get better answers.
keyTakeaways: Grok is available inside X and through its own app and website; access depends on your plan.
Its edge is real-time information from X and the web, plus a more casual, conversational style.
Ask clear questions and lean on its live strengths — "what's happening with X right now" type queries.
For careful, high-stakes work, mainstream assistants are often steadier, and you should verify anything important.
description: How to use Grok, xAI's chatbot: where to access it, its real-time strengths, prompting tips, and when to reach for it versus ChatGPT or Claude.

sections.heading: How to start using Grok
sections.html: Grok is xAI's AI assistant, and the quickest way in is through X (formerly Twitter), where it's built in; it also has its own app and website. Open it, type a question in plain language, and refine from there — it works like any chat assistant. Access and limits depend on your plan, so check the current options. New to what it is? Start with what is Grok.

sections.heading: What Grok is best at
sections.html:
• Real-time questions — trending topics and live discussion on X, where most assistants lag.
  • Casual conversation — a looser, more irreverent tone if other assistants feel stiff.
  • Quick takes — fast, conversational answers on what's happening now.

For long documents, precise reasoning, or sensitive work, steadier all-rounders like ChatGPT and Claude are often the better call.

sections.heading: How to get better answers from Grok
sections.html: The same fundamentals that work everywhere work here: give it a role, the context, the task, and the format you want. Lean into its real-time strength by being specific about timeframe and source ("summarize what people on X are saying about X today"). And because any model can be wrong or biased, verify anything that matters. For the full method, see how to write a good prompt.

sections.heading: When to use Grok vs other tools
sections.html: Reach for Grok when you want a live read on a trending topic or a casual back-and-forth; keep a polished all-rounder as your default for general work. The real skill isn't picking one tool — it's directing whichever you use well. That's AI fluency, and you can build it in about 5 minutes a day. See the wider field in the best AI apps.

faq.q: How do I start using Grok?
faq.a: The fastest way is inside X (formerly Twitter), where Grok is built in; it also has its own app and website. Open it, ask a question in plain language, and refine. Access and limits depend on your plan.
Grok is available inside X and via its own app and site, with access and limits that depend on your plan and any X subscription. Check the current options, as they change.

Real-time questions about trending topics and live discussion on X, plus a casual conversational style. For careful, high-stakes work, mainstream assistants like ChatGPT and Claude are often steadier.

faq.a: Use the same prompting fundamentals that work everywhere: give a role, context, the task, and the format you want. Lean on its real-time strength by being specific about timeframe and source, and verify anything important.
